title: chaenactis glabriuscula var. orcuttiana
intro: Chaenactis glabriuscula var. orcuttiana, also known as Orcutt's woolly whitlow-grass, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that aid in soothing skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits associated with Chaenactis glabriuscula var. orcuttiana include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
